crossbeam-utils/src/sync/parker.rs ... (truncated at 200 entries) ## README.md # Crossbeam [![Build Status](https://github.com/crossbeam-rs/crossbeam/workflows/CI/badge.svg)]( https://github.com/crossbeam-rs/crossbeam/actions) [![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT_OR_Apache--2.0-blue.svg)]( https://github.com/crossbeam-rs/crossbeam#license) [![Cargo](https://img.shields.io/crates/v/crossbeam.svg)]( https://crates.io/crates/crossbeam) [![Documentation](https://docs.rs/crossbeam/badge.svg)]( https://docs.rs/crossbeam) [![Rust 1.74+](https://img.shields.io/badge/rust-1.74+-lightgray.svg)]( https://www.rust-lang.org) [![chat](https://img.shields.io/discord/569610676205781012.svg?logo=discord)](https://discord.com/invite/JXYwgWZ) This crate provides a set of tools for concurrent programming: #### Atomics * [`AtomicCell`], a thread-safe mutable memory location.(no_std) * [`AtomicConsume`], for reading from primitive atomic types with "consume" ordering.(no_std) #### Data structures * [`deque`], work-stealing deques for building task schedulers. * [`ArrayQueue`], a bounded MPMC queue that allocates a fixed-capacity buffer on construction.(alloc) * [`SegQueue`], an unbounded MPMC queue that allocates small buffers, segments, on demand.(alloc) #### Memory management * [`epoch`], an epoch-based garbage collector.(alloc) #### Thread synchronization * [`channel`], multi-producer multi-consumer channels for message passing. * [`Parker`], a thread parking primitive. * [`ShardedLock`], a sharded reader-writer lock with fast concurrent reads. * [`WaitGroup`], for synchronizing the beginning or end of some computation. #### Utilities * [`Backoff`], for exponential backoff in spin loops.(no_std) * [`CachePadded`], for padding and aligning a value to the length of a cache line.(no_std) * [`scope`], for spawning threads that borrow local variables from the stack. *Features marked with (no_std) can be used in `no_std` environments.*
*Features marked with (alloc) can be used in `no_std` environments, but only if `alloc` feature is enabled.* [`AtomicCell`]: https://docs.rs/crossbeam/latest/crossbeam/atomic/struct.AtomicCell.html [`AtomicConsume`]: https://docs.rs/crossbeam/latest/crossbeam/atomic/trait.AtomicConsume.html [`deque`]: https://docs.rs/crossbeam/latest/crossbeam/deque/index.html [`ArrayQueue`]: https://docs.rs/crossbeam/latest/crossbeam/queue/struct.ArrayQueue.html [`SegQueue`]: https://docs.rs/crossbeam/latest/crossbeam/queue/struct.SegQueue.html [`channel`]: https://docs.rs/crossbeam/latest/crossbeam/channel/index.html [`Parker`]: https://docs.rs/crossbeam/latest/crossbeam/sync/struct.Parker.html [`ShardedLock`]: https://docs.rs/crossbeam/latest/crossbeam/sync/struct.ShardedLock.html [`WaitGroup`]: https://docs.rs/crossbeam/latest/crossbeam/sync/struct.WaitGroup.html [`epoch`]: https://docs.rs/crossbeam/latest/crossbeam/epoch/index.html [`Backoff`]: https://docs.rs/crossbeam/latest/crossbeam/utils/struct.Backoff.html [`CachePadded`]: https://docs.rs/crossbeam/latest/crossbeam/utils/struct.CachePadded.html [`scope`]: https://docs.rs/crossbeam/latest/crossbeam/fn.scope.html ## Crates The main `crossbeam` crate just [re-exports](src/lib.rs) tools from smaller subcrates: * [`crossbeam-channel`](crossbeam-channel) provides multi-producer multi-consumer channels for message passing. * [`crossbeam-deque`](crossbeam-deque) provides work-stealing deques, which are primarily intended for building task schedulers. * [`crossbeam-epoch`](crossbeam-epoch) provides epoch-based garbage collection for building concurrent data structures. * [`crossbeam-queue`](crossbeam-queue) provides concurrent queues that can be shared among threads. * [`crossbeam-utils`](crossbeam-utils) provides atomics, synchronization primitives, scoped threads, and other utilities. There is one more experimental subcrate that is not yet included in `crossbeam`: * [`crossbeam-skiplist`](crossbeam-skiplist) provides concurrent maps and sets based on lock-free skip lists. ## Usage Add this to your `Cargo.toml`: ```toml [dependencies] crossbeam = "0.8" ``` ## Compatibility Crossbeam supports stable Rust releases going back at least one year, and every time the minimum supported Rust version is increased, a new minor version is released. Currently, the minimum supported Rust version is 1.74. ## Contributing Crossbeam welcomes contribution from everyone in the form of suggestions, bug reports, pull requests, and feedback. 💛 If you need ideas for contribution, there are several ways to get started: * Found a bug or have a feature request? [Submit an issue](https://github.com/crossbeam-rs/crossbeam/issues/new)! * Issues and PRs labeled with [feedback wanted](https://github.com/crossbeam-rs/crossbeam/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aopen+sort%3Aupdated-desc+label%3A%22feedback+wanted%22+) need feedback from users and contributors. * Issues labeled with [good first issue](https://github.com/crossbeam-rs/crossbeam/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aopen+sort%3Aupdated-desc+label%3A%22good+first+issue%22) are relatively easy starter issues. #### RFCs We also have the [RFCs](https://github.com/crossbeam-rs/rfcs) repository for more high-level discussion, which is the place where we brainstorm ideas and propose substantial changes to Crossbeam. You are welcome to participate in any open [issues](https://github.com/crossbeam-rs/rfcs/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aopen+sort%3Aupdated-desc) or [pull requests](https://github.com/crossbeam-rs/rfcs/pulls?q=is%3Apr+is%3Aopen+sort%3Aupdated-desc). #### Learning resources If you'd like to learn more about concurrency and non-blocking data structures, there's a list of learning resources in our [wiki](https://github.com/crossbeam-rs/rfcs/wiki), which includes relevant blog posts, papers, videos, and other similar projects. Another good place to visit is [merged RFCs](https://github.com/crossbeam-rs/rfcs/tree/HEAD/text). They contain elaborate descriptions and rationale for features we've introduced to Crossbeam, but keep in mind that some of the written information is now out of date. #### Conduct The Crossbeam project adheres to the [Rust Code of Conduct](https://www.rust-lang.org/policies/code-of-conduct). This describes the minimum behavior expected from all contributors. ## License Licensed under either of * Apache License, Version 2.0 ([LICENSE-APACHE](LICENSE-APACHE) or http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0) * MIT license ([LICENSE-MIT](LICENSE-MIT) or http://opensource.org/licenses/MIT) at your option. Some Crossbeam subcrates have additional licensing notices. Take a look at other readme files in this repository for more information. #### Contribution Unless you explicitly state otherwise, any contribution intentionally submitted for inclusion in the work by you, as defined in the Apache-2.0 license, shall be dual licensed as above, without any additional terms or conditions. Use `read` and `bash` tools at the path above to explore further.

## README.md
# Notify

[![» Crate](https://flat.badgen.net/crates/v/notify)][crate]
[![» Docs](https://flat.badgen.net/badge/api/docs.rs/df3600)][notify-docs]
[![» CI](https://flat.badgen.net/github/checks/notify-rs/notify/main)][build]
[![» Downloads](https://flat.badgen.net/crates/d/notify)][crate]
[![» Conduct](https://flat.badgen.net/badge/contributor/covenant/5e0d73)][coc]
[![» Public Domain](https://flat.badgen.net/badge/license/CC0-1.0/purple)][cc0]

_Cross-platform filesystem notification library for Rust._


- [Notify Documentation][notify-docs]
- [Notify Types Documentation][notify-types-docs]
- [Mini Debouncer Documentation][debouncer-mini-docs]
- [Full Debouncer Documentation][debouncer-full-docs]
- [File ID][file-id-docs]
- [Examples][examples]
- [Changelog][changelog]
- [Upgrading notify from v8](./docs/UPGRADING_V8_TO_V9.md)
- [Upgrading notify from v4](./docs/UPGRADING_V4_TO_V5.md)
- Minimum supported Rust version: **1.88**

As used by: [alacritty], [cargo watch], [cobalt], [deno], [docket], [mdBook],
[rust-analyzer], [watchexec], [watchfiles], [xi-editor], [zed]
and others.

(Looking for desktop notifications instead? Have a look at [notify-rust] or
[alert-after]!)

## Minimum Supported Rust Version (MSRV) Policy

We follow these MSRV rules:

- The current MSRV is **1.88**.
- MSRV bumps do NOT require a major release and may happen in minor releases.
- The MSRV may be updated when needed, but support for the current stable Rust release and the previous two stable releases (N, N-1, N-2) is always guaranteed.
  - For example, if the current stable version is 1.85, we guarantee support for 1.85, 1.84, and 1.83, so the minimum supported Rust version will be **at most** 1.83.
- MSRV is bumped only when needed, e.g.:
  - required by dependencies
  - any serious bug is found (including security-related)
  - adopting new stable Rust features
- Every MSRV bump is documented in the release notes when it happens.

## Platforms

- Linux / Android: inotify
- macOS: FSEvents or kqueue, see features
- Windows: ReadDirectoryChangesW
- iOS / FreeBSD / NetBSD / OpenBSD / DragonflyBSD: kqueue
- All platforms: polling

## License

notify is licensed under the [CC Zero 1.0][cc0].    
notify-types is licensed under the [MIT] or [Apache-2.0][apache] license.  
notify-debouncer-mini is licensed under the [MIT] or [Apache-2.0][apache] license.  
notify-debouncer-full is licensed under the [MIT] or [Apache-2.0][apache] license.  
file-id is licensed under the [MIT] or [Apache-2.0][apache] license.

## Origins

Inspired by Go's [fsnotify] and Node.js's [Chokidar], born out of need for
[cargo watch], and general frustration at the non-existence of C/Rust
cross-platform notify libraries.

Originally created by [Félix Saparelli] and awesome [contributors].

[Chokidar]: https://github.com/paulmillr/chokidar
[FileSystemEventSecurity]: https://developer.apple.com/library/mac/documentation/Darwin/Conceptual/FSEvents_ProgGuide/FileSystemEventSecurity/FileSystemEventSecurity.html
[debouncer-full-docs]: https://docs.rs/notify-debouncer-full/latest/notify_debouncer_full/
[debouncer-mini-docs]: https://docs.rs/notify-debouncer-mini/latest/notify_debouncer_mini/
[Félix Saparelli]: https://passcod.name

## README.md
# mlua
[![Build Status]][github-actions] [![Latest Version]][crates.io] [![API Documentation]][docs.rs] [![Coverage Status]][codecov.io] ![MSRV]

[Build Status]: https://github.com/mlua-rs/mlua/workflows/CI/badge.svg
[github-actions]: https://github.com/mlua-rs/mlua/actions
[Latest Version]: https://img.shields.io/crates/v/mlua.svg
[crates.io]: https://crates.io/crates/mlua
[API Documentation]: https://docs.rs/mlua/badge.svg
[docs.rs]: https://docs.rs/mlua
[Coverage Status]: https://codecov.io/gh/mlua-rs/mlua/branch/main/graph/badge.svg?token=99339FS1CG
[codecov.io]: https://codecov.io/gh/mlua-rs/mlua
[MSRV]: https://img.shields.io/badge/rust-1.88+-brightgreen.svg?&logo=rust

[Guided Tour] | [Benchmarks] | [FAQ]

[Guided Tour]: examples/guided_tour.rs
[Benchmarks]: https://github.com/khvzak/script-bench-rs
[FAQ]: FAQ.md

`mlua` is a set of bindings to the [Lua](https://www.lua.org) programming language for Rust with a goal of providing a
_safe_ (as much as possible), high level, easy to use, practical and flexible API.

Started as an `rlua` fork, `mlua` supports Lua 5.5, 5.4, 5.3, 5.2, 5.1 (including LuaJIT) and [Luau] and allows writing native Lua modules in Rust as well as using Lua in a standalone mode.

`mlua` is tested on Windows/macOS/Linux including module mode in [GitHub Actions] on `x86_64` platforms and cross-compilation to `aarch64` (other targets are also supported).

WebAssembly (WASM) is supported through the `wasm32-unknown-emscripten` target for all Lua/Luau versions excluding JIT.

[GitHub Actions]: https://github.com/mlua-rs/mlua/actions
[Luau]: https://luau.org

## Usage

### Feature flags

`mlua` uses feature flags to reduce the number of dependencies and compiled code, and allow choosing only the required set of features.
Below is a list of the available feature flags. By default `mlua` does not enable any features.

* `lua55`: enable Lua [5.5] support
* `lua54`: enable Lua [5.4] support
* `lua53`: enable Lua [5.3] support
* `lua52`: enable Lua [5.2] support
* `lua51`: enable Lua [5.1] support
* `luajit`: enable [LuaJIT] support
* `luajit52`: enable [LuaJIT] support with partial compatibility with Lua 5.2
* `luau`: enable [Luau] support (auto vendored mode)
* `luau-jit`: enable [Luau] support with JIT backend.
* `luau-vector4`: enable [Luau] support with 4-dimensional vector.
* `vendored`: build static Lua(JIT) libraries from sources during `mlua` compilation using [lua-src] or [luajit-src]
* `module`: enable module mode (building loadable `cdylib` library for Lua)
* `async`: enable async/await support (any executor can be used, eg. [tokio] or [async-std])
* `send`: make `mlua::Lua: Send + Sync` (adds [`Send`] requirement to `mlua::Function` and `Send + Sync` to `mlua::UserData`)
* `error-send`: make `mlua::Error: Send + Sync`
* `serde`: add serialization and deserialization support to `mlua` types using [serde]
* `macros`: enable procedural macros (such as `chunk!`)
* `anyhow`: enable `anyhow::Error` conversion into Lua
* `userdata-wrappers`: opt into `impl UserData` for `Rc<T>`/`Arc<T>`/`Rc<RefCell<T>>`/`Arc<Mutex<T>>` where `T: UserData`

[5.5]: https://www.lua.org/manual/5.5/manual.html
[5.4]: https://www.lua.org/manual/5.4/manual.html
[5.3]: https://www.lua.org/manual/5.3/manual.html
[5.2]: https://www.lua.org/manual/5.2/manual.html
[5.1]: https://www.lua.org/manual/5.1/manual.html
[LuaJIT]: https://luajit.org/
[Luau]: https://github.com/luau-lang/luau
[lua-src]: https://github.com/mlua-rs/lua-src-rs
[luajit-src]: https://github.com/mlua-rs/luajit-src-rs
[tokio]: https://github.com/tokio-rs/tokio
[async-std]: https://github.com/async-rs/async-std
[`Send`]: https://doc.rust-lang.org/std/marker/trait.Send.html
[serde]: https://github.com/serde-rs/serde

### Async/await support

`mlua` supports async/await for all Lua versions including Luau.

This works using Lua [coroutines](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.3/manual.html#2.6) and requires running [Thread](https://docs.rs/mlua/latest/mlua/struct.Thread.html) along with enabling `feature = "async"` in `Cargo.toml`.

**Examples**:
- [HTTP Client](examples/async_http_client.rs)
- [HTTP Client (json)](examples/async_http_reqwest.rs)
- [HTTP Server](examples/async_http_server.rs)
- [TCP Server](examples/async_tcp_server.rs)


**shell command examples**:
```shell
# async http client (hyper)
cargo run --example async_http_client --features=lua54,async,macros

# async http client (reqwest)
cargo run --example async_http_reqwest --features=lua54,async,macros,serde

# async http server
cargo run --example async_http_server --features=lua54,async,macros,send
curl -v http://localhost:3000
```

### Serde support

With the `serde` feature flag enabled, `mlua` allows you to serialize/deserialize any type that implements [`serde::Serialize`] and [`serde::Deserialize`] into/from [`mlua::Value`]. In addition, `mlua` provides the [`serde::Serialize`] trait implementation for `mlua::Value` (including `UserData` support).

[Example](examples/serde.rs)

[`serde::Serialize`]: https://docs.serde.rs/serde/ser/trait.Serialize.html
[`serde::Deserialize`]: https://docs.serde.rs/serde/de/trait.Deserialize.html
[`mlua::Value`]: https://docs.rs/mlua/latest/mlua/enum.Value.html

### Compiling

You have to enable one of the features: `lua54`, `lua53`, `lua52`, `lua51`, `luajit(52)` or `luau`, according to the chosen Lua version.

By default `mlua` uses `pkg-config` to find Lua includes and libraries for the chosen Lua version.
In most cases it works as desired, although sometimes it may be preferable to use a custom Lua library.
To achieve this, mlua supports the `LUA_LIB`, `LUA_LIB_NAME` and `LUA_LINK` environment variables.
`LUA_LINK` is optional and may be `dylib` (a dynamic library) or `static` (a static library, `.a` archive).

An example of how to use them:
``` sh
my_project $ LUA_LIB=$HOME/tmp/lua-5.2.4/src LUA_LIB_NAME=lua LUA_LINK=static cargo build
```

`mlua` also supports vendored Lua/LuaJIT using the auxiliary crates [lua-src](https://crates.io/crates/lua-src) and
[luajit-src](https://crates.io/crates/luajit-src).
Just enable the `vendored` feature and cargo will automatically build and link the specified Lua/LuaJIT version. This is the easiest way to get started with `mlua`.

### Standalone mode
In standalone mode, `mlua` allows adding scripting support to your application with a properly configured Lua runtime to ensure safety and soundness.

Add to `Cargo.toml`:

``` toml
[dependencies]
mlua = { version = "0.12", features = ["lua54", "vendored"] }
```

`main.rs`

``` rust
use mlua::prelude::*;

fn main() -> LuaResult<()> {
    let lua = Lua::new();

    let map_table = lua.create_table()?;
    map_table.set(1, "one")?;
    map_table.set("two", 2)?;

    lua.globals().set("map_table", map_table)?;

    lua.load("for k,v in pairs(map_table) do print(k,v) end").exec()?;

    Ok(())
}
```

### Module mode
In module mode, `mlua` allows creating a compiled Lua module that can be loaded from Lua code using [`require`](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.4/manual.html#pdf-require). In this case `mlua` uses an external Lua runtime which could lead to potential unsafety due to the unpredictability of the Lua environment and usage of libraries such as [`debug`](https://www.lua.org/manual/5.4/manual.html#6.10).

[Example](examples/module)

Add to `Cargo.toml`:

``` toml
[lib]
crate-type = ["cdylib"]

[dependencies]
mlua = { version = "0.12", features = ["lua54", "module"] }
```

`lib.rs`:

``` rust
use mlua::prelude::*;

fn hello(_: &Lua, name: String) -> LuaResult<()> {
    println!("hello, {}!", name);
    Ok(())
}

#[mlua::lua_module]
fn my_module(lua: &Lua) -> LuaResult<LuaTable> {
    let exports = lua.create_table()?;
    exports.set("hello", lua.create_function(hello)?)?;
    Ok(exports)
}
```

And then (**macOS** example):

``` sh
$ cargo rustc -- -C link-arg=-undefined -C link-arg=dynamic_lookup
$ ln -s ./target/debug/libmy_module.dylib ./my_module.so
$ lua5.4 -e 'require("my_module").hello("world")'
hello, world!
```

On macOS, you need to set additional linker arguments. One option is to compile with `cargo rustc --release -- -C link-arg=-undefined -C link-arg=dynamic_lookup`, the other is t

## README.md
# nvim-rs ![CI](https://github.com/KillTheMule/nvim-rs/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)  [![(Docs.rs)](https://docs.rs/nvim-rs/badge.svg)](https://docs.rs/nvim-rs/) [![(Crates.io status)](https://img.shields.io/crates/v/nvim-rs.svg)](https://crates.io/crates/nvim-rs)
Rust library for Neovim msgpack-rpc clients. Utilizes async to allow for arbitrary nesting of requests.

## Status

Useable, see the `examples/` and `tests/` folders for examples. The `nvim_rs::examples` submodule contains documentation of the examples.

The **API** is unstable, see the [Roadmap](https://github.com/KillTheMule/nvim-rs/issues/1) for things being planned.

## Contributing

I'd love contributions, comments, praise, criticism... You could open an [issue](https://github.com/KillTheMule/nvim-rs/issues) or a [pull request](https://github.com/KillTheMule/nvim-rs/pulls). I also read the subreddits for [rust](https://www.reddit.com/r/rust/), if that suits you better.

## Running tests

For some tests, neovim needs to be installed. Set the environment variable `NVIMRS_TEST_BIN` to
the path of the binary before running the tests.

Afterwards, you can simply run `cargo test --features="use_tokio"`.
Also run `cargo build --examples --features="use_tokio"` as well as `cargo
bench -- --test --features="use_tokio"` to make sure everything still compiles
(replace `use_tokio` by `use_async-std` to do all the above with `async-std`
instead of `tokio`). 

## License

As this is a fork of [neovim-lib](https://github.com/daa84/neovim-lib), it is licensed under the GNU Lesser General Public License v3.0.

**IMPORTANT**: All commits to this project, including all PRs, are
dual-licensed under the Apache or MIT license. This is to allow the possibility
of relicensing this project later.

## CoC

Wherever applicable, this project follows the [rust code of
conduct](https://www.rust-lang.org/en-US/conduct.html).

- Rust loadable LuaJIT module mlua module feature Neovim require cdylib: Started as an `rlua` fork, `mlua` supports Lua 5.5, 5.4, 5.3, 5.2, 5.1 (including LuaJIT) and [Luau] and allows writing native Lua modules in Rust as well as using Lua in a standalone mode. ... * `luajit`: enable [LuaJIT] support * ... *...
- Rust Neovim plugin without nvim-oxi mlua LuaJIT module: TLDR: You can use rust with a library like mlua to compile a cdylib shared library that is also a valid lua module which then can be loaded by neovim. The link to the git tree for all the code in this blog post is here. ... ovim v0.5.0 i...
- Rust Lua module background thread event queue polling LuaJIT Neovim architecture: void loop_init(Loop *loop, void *data) { uv_loop_init(&loop->uv); loop->recursive = 0; loop->closing = false; loop->uv.data = loop; kv_init(loop->children); loop->events = multiqueue_new(loop_on_put, loop); loop->fast_events = multiqueue...

Completed queries: 3
Successful: 3
Failed: 0

Sources
- https://github.com/mlua-rs/mlua
- https://kdheepak.com/blog/loading-a-rust-library-as-a-lua-module-in-neovim/
- https://docs.rs/crate/mlua/latest
- https://crates.io/crates/mlua
- https://github.com/mlua-rs/mlua/blob/main/README.md
- https://lib.rs/crates/mlua

- https://github.com/turboladen/overkill_nvim
- https://docs.rs/nvim-oxi/latest/nvim_oxi/
- https://neovim.io/doc/user/lua-plugin/
- ... and 11 more

examples/module/Cargo.toml:18:luajit = ["mlua/luajit"]
Cargo.toml:14:High level bindings to Lua 5.5/5.4/5.3/5.2/5.1 (including LuaJIT) and Luau
Cargo.toml:34:luajit = ["ffi/luajit"]
Cargo.toml:40:module = ["mlua_derive", "ffi/module"]
Cargo.toml:41:async = ["dep:futures-util"]
Cargo.toml:42:send = ["error-send"]
README.md:23:Started as an `rlua` fork, `mlua` supports Lua 5.5, 5.4, 5.3, 5.2, 5.1 (including LuaJIT) and [Luau] and allows writing native Lua modules in Rust as well as using Lua in a standalone mode.
README.md:44:* `luajit`: enable [LuaJIT] support
README.md:45:* `luajit52`: enable [LuaJIT] support with partial compatibility with Lua 5.2
README.md:64:[LuaJIT]: https://luajit.org/
README.md:123:`mlua` also supports vendored Lua/LuaJIT using the auxiliary crates [lua-src](https://crates.io/crates/lua-src) and
README.md:125:Just enable the `vendored` feature and cargo will automatically build and link the specified Lua/LuaJIT version. This is the easiest way to get started with `mlua`.
README.md:157:### Module mode
README.md:182:#[mlua::lua_module]
examples/module/src/lib.rs:11:#[mlua::lua_module]

Cargo.toml:6:description = "A library for writing neovim rpc clients"
Cargo.toml:10:keywords = ["neovim", "nvim", "rpc", "msgpack"]
Cargo.toml:23:use_tokio = ["tokio", "tokio-util"]
Cargo.toml:28:features = ["use_tokio"]
Cargo.toml:39:tokio = { version = "1.52.3", features = ["full", "net"] , optional = true}
Cargo.toml:40:tokio-util = { version = "0.7.18", features = ["compat"], optional = true }
Cargo.toml:57:name = "rpc_tokio"
Cargo.toml:59:required-features = ["use_tokio"]
Cargo.toml:63:required-features = ["use_tokio"]
Cargo.toml:67:required-features = ["use_tokio"]
Cargo.toml:71:required-features = ["use_tokio"]
Cargo.toml:75:required-features = ["use_tokio"]
Cargo.toml:78:name = "bench_tokio"
Cargo.toml:79:required-features = ["use_tokio"]
Cargo.toml:95:required-features = ["use_tokio"]
Cargo.toml:99:required-features = ["use_tokio"]
Cargo.toml:107:required-features = ["use_tokio"]
Cargo.toml:118:required-features = ["use_tokio"]
README.md:2:Rust library for Neovim msgpack-rpc clients. Utilizes async to allow for arbitrary nesting of requests.
README.md:8:The **API** is unstable, see the [Roadmap](https://github.com/KillTheMule/nvim-rs/issues/1) for things being planned.
README.md:19:Afterwards, you can simply run `cargo test --features="use_tokio"`.
README.md:20:Also run `cargo build --examples --features="use_tokio"` as well as `cargo
README.md:21:bench -- --test --features="use_tokio"` to make sure everything still compiles
README.md:22:(replace `use_tokio` by `use_async-std` to do all the above with `async-std`
README.md:23:instead of `tokio`).

arguments.command: cd /tmp/pi-github-repos/mlua-rs/mlua && git log -1 --date=short --format='%ad %h %s' && git tag --sort=-v:refname | head -10; printf '\n--- nvim-oxi ---\n'; cd /tmp/pi-github-repos/noib3/nvim-oxi && git log -1 --date=short --format='%ad %h %s' && git tag --sort=-v:refname | head -10

content.text:
Registers Lua module entrypoint.

You can register multiple entrypoints as required.

```rust,ignore
use mlua::{Lua, Result, Table};

#[mlua::lua_module]
fn my_module(lua: &Lua) -> Result<Table> {
    let exports = lua.create_table()?;
    exports.set("hello", "world")?;
    Ok(exports)
}
```

Internally in the code above the compiler defines C function `luaopen_my_module`.

You can also pass options to the attribute:

* name - name of the module, defaults to the name of the function

```rust,ignore
#[mlua::lua_module(name = "alt_module")]
fn my_module(lua: &Lua) -> Result<Table> {
    ...
}
```

* skip_memory_check - skip memory allocation checks for some operations.

In module mode, mlua runs in an unknown environment and cannot tell whether there are any memory
limits or not. As a result, some operations that require memory allocation run in protected
mode. Setting this attribute will improve performance of such operations with risk of having
uncaught exceptions and memory leaks.

```rust,ignore
#[mlua::lua_module(skip_memory_check)]
fn my_module(lua: &Lua) -> Result<Table> {
    ...
}
```

[package]
name = "rust_module"
version = "0.0.0"
authors = ["Aleksandr Orlenko <zxteam@pm.me>"]
edition = "2021"

[lib]
crate-type = ["cdylib"]

[workspace]

[features]
lua55 = ["mlua/lua55"]
lua54 = ["mlua/lua54"]
lua53 = ["mlua/lua53"]
lua52 = ["mlua/lua52"]
lua51 = ["mlua/lua51"]
luajit = ["mlua/luajit"]

[dependencies]
mlua = { path = "../..", features = ["module"] }

arguments.command:
set -e
mkdir -p /tmp/pi-lib-research
(
  test -d /tmp/pi-lib-research/crossbeam || git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/crossbeam-rs/crossbeam.git /tmp/pi-lib-research/crossbeam
) &
(
  test -d /tmp/pi-lib-research/flume || git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/zkat/flume.git /tmp/pi-lib-research/flume
) &
(
  test -d /tmp/pi-lib-research/notify || git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/notify-rs/notify.git /tmp/pi-lib-research/notify
) &
(
  test -d /tmp/pi-lib-research/ignore || git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/BurntSushi/ripgrep.git /tmp/pi-lib-research/ignore
) &
wait
printf '%s\n' done

researcher:
# Research: Rust filesystem/domain core connected to Lua in Neovim

## Summary

For **fred.nvim**, the best fit is an in-process Rust `cdylib` exposed as a Lua module with **mlua**, while keeping a deliberately narrow API such as `start_scan()`, `poll_results()`, `cancel()`, and watcher lifecycle functions. Lua should create every timer, drain results on Neovim’s main thread, and perform every `vim.api`, UI, and event-loop operation; Rust workers should communicate only through bounded queues containing owned Rust data.

A raw LuaJIT FFI/C ABI can reduce binding overhead and remove the Lua C API dependency, but it creates substantially more unsafe ABI, ownership, packaging, and panic-handling work. A sidecar is attractive when crash isolation matters, but under the stated ownership rule a simple Lua-owned stdio protocol is a cleaner fit than using most of `nvim-rs`.

## Findings

1. **mlua is the strongest maintained in-process binding.**  
   `mlua` 0.12 was published in July 2026, supports Lua 5.1, LuaJIT, native loadable modules, async integration, userdata, and Serde conversion, and tests module mode on Windows, macOS, and Linux. The relevant fred.nvim configuration is approximately:

   ```toml
   [lib]
   crate-type = ["cdylib"]

   [dependencies]
   mlua = {
     version = "0.12",
     features = ["luajit", "module", "macros"]
   }
   ```

   Add `serde` only if structured Lua-table conversion is useful. Do **not** enable `vendored` for a module loaded into Neovim: module mode must operate on the host Lua state rather than introducing an independently linked Lua runtime. The `send` feature is also inappropriate for the host Lua state in module mode; Lua values and references should remain on the calling Neovim thread. [mlua repository and feature documentation](https://github.com/mlua-rs/mlua) [mlua crate documentation](https://docs.rs/mlua/latest/mlua/)

2. **mlua fits the ownership constraint if its public surface is kept domain-only.**  
   An mlua module does not inherently call Neovim. Rust can expose ordinary Lua-callable functions returning tables, userdata, IDs, status values, and errors without ever looking up `vim`, invoking Lua callbacks from worker threads, or touching `vim.api`. The boundary should be:

   - Lua → Rust: submit immutable scan/search/watch configuration.
   - Rust → bounded queue: send owned result DTOs.
   - Lua → Rust: call a nonblocking `poll_results(max_items)`.
   - Lua: transform results into buffers, extmarks, floats, notifications, or other UI.
   - Lua: own `vim.uv` timers and wrap fast-event callbacks with `vim.schedule_wrap`.

   Neovim explicitly requires editor operations from fast/libuv contexts to be scheduled onto the main loop, and OS-level threads cannot directly access editor state. [Neovim Lua event-loop and threading documentation](https://neovim.io/doc/user/lua/#lua-loop) [Neovim scheduling/API rules](https://neovim.io/doc/user/api/#api-fast)

3. **Loadable-module packaging is practical but requires platform artifacts.**  
   Neovim searches `runtimepath` for native modules such as `lua/fred_native.so` or `.dll`, derived from `package.cpath`, so the binary can be distributed alongside the Lua plugin. macOS normally requires unresolved Lua symbols to use dynamic lookup, and output names/extensions need platform-specific handling. A release therefore needs at least per-OS and per-architecture builds, plus a Lua loader that reports unsupported triples cleanly. [Neovim native module search rules](https://neovim.io/doc/user/lua/#lua-module-load) [mlua module example](https://github.com/mlua-rs/mlua/tree/main/examples/module)

4. **LuaJIT cannot be assumed for every Neovim build.**  
   Neovim’s permanent Lua compatibility interface is Lua 5.1, while LuaJIT is recommended on supported platforms but is not mandatory. A module compiled specifically with mlua’s `luajit` feature, and especially a solution using `require("ffi")`, should either declare LuaJIT as a fred.nvim requirement or provide a fallback/pure-Lua path. Testing a separate `lua51,module` build may improve coverage, but native-runtime ABI compatibility must be verified against actual supported Neovim builds rather than assumed. [Neovim Lua compatibility statement](https://neovim.io/doc/user/lua/#lua-compat) [Neovim LuaJIT availability guidance](https://neovim.io/doc/user/lua/#lua-luajit)

5. **Raw LuaJIT FFI with a small Rust C ABI is viable, but lower-level.**  
   Rust can compile a `cdylib` exporting functions such as:

   ```c
   fred_handle *fred_start_scan(const fred_scan_options *);
   size_t fred_poll(fred_handle *, fred_result *out, size_t capacity);
   void fred_cancel(fred_handle *);
   void fred_result_free(fred_result *);
   void fred_handle_free(fred_handle *);
   ```

   LuaJIT can load it with `ffi.load()` and declare the interface with `ffi.cdef()`. This avoids writing Lua C-stack code and permits a very narrow, stable domain boundary. LuaJIT’s official documentation specifically positions FFI as a way to call external C functions and manipulate C structures without hand-written Lua/C bindings. [LuaJIT FFI documentation](https://github.com/LuaJIT/LuaJIT/blob/v2.1/doc/ext_ffi.html)

   The costs are significant:

   - Only C-compatible `#[repr(C)]` layouts may cross the boundary.
   - Strings and arrays need explicit pointer/length and ownership rules.
   - Every allocation requires an unambiguous matching free function.
   - Handles need generation/liveness checks to prevent use-after-free.
   - Rust panics must be caught before crossing `extern "C"`.
   - LuaJIT FFI is unavailable on non-LuaJIT Neovim builds.
   - ABI evolution needs explicit version functions or versioned symbol sets.
   - Lua must avoid holding raw pointers after shutdown or module reload.

   `cbindgen` can generate C declarations from Rust’s public C API, although its maintainers describe development as ad hoc and note that unsupported constructs may require contributor work. [cbindgen repository](https://github.com/mozilla/cbindgen)

6. **Existing standalone LuaJIT sys crates are weaker than mlua/mlua-sys.**  
   `luajit2-sys` is still at 0.0.2 from 2020 and its generated constants refer to LuaJIT 2.1 beta 3; `lua-jit-sys` targets LuaJIT 2.0.5 and dates to 2017. They are poor foundations for a new 2026 plugin. If low-level access to the Lua C API is unavoidable, the actively released **mlua-sys** is more credible, but its own documentation says it is intended to be consumed by mlua, so directly adopting it means accepting unsafe Lua-stack management without much benefit for fred.nvim. [luajit2-sys](https://crates.io/crates/luajit2-sys) [lua-jit-sys](https://crates.io/crates/lua-jit-sys) [mlua-sys](https://crates.io/crates/mlua-sys)

7. **nvim-rs remains maintained, but its primary abstraction conflicts with the boundary.**  
   `nvim-rs` is an async MessagePack-RPC client for Neovim, supporting Tokio and async-std/smol-style integrations and arbitrary nesting of requests. Its repository was pushed in January 2026; the current published line is 0.9.2, with the README still describing the public API as unstable. Relevant features include `use_tokio`, `use_async-std`, and `use_smol`. [nvim-rs repository](https://github.com/KillTheMule/nvim-rs) [nvim-rs documentation](https://docs.rs/nvim-rs/latest/nvim_rs/)

   The normal `nvim-rs` design gives Rust a `Neovim` client used to issue `nvim_*` requests. That directly violates “Rust must not call Neovim APIs.” It could technically be restricted to handling custom inbound requests while never using the client handle, but that discards much of the crate’s value and leaves an easy architectural escape hatch.

8. **If a sidecar is selected, use a Lua-owned custom transport rather than a Neovim client abstraction.**  
   A clean sidecar model is:

   - Lua starts the binary using `vim.system`, `vim.uv.spawn`, or a Lua-owned job.
   - Lua sends newline-delimited JSON, length-prefixed MessagePack, or another private protocol.
   - Rust performs scans/watches and writes result messages.
   - Lua receives stdout in its libuv callbacks, schedules processing, and performs all Neovim operations.

   `serde_json` is simplest; `rmp-serde` is a reasonable compact MessagePack option. This gives Rust no Neovim channel or API client at all, making the ownership rule mechanically clearer than a restricted `nvim-rs` instance.

   Sidecar advantages are process crash isolation, independent restarts/upgrades, no Lua ABI linkage, and simpler support for non-LuaJIT Neovim. Costs include process lifecycle management, framing/backpressure, serialization overhead, cancellation protocol design, and less convenient synchronous domain calls.

9. **nvim-oxi is credible and active, but explicitly unsuitable for this architecture.**  
   `nvim-oxi` had repository activity in April–June 2026 and provides typed in-process Neovim bindings. Its defining feature is directly hooking Rust into Neovim C functions, avoiding RPC. That is exactly what the requested architecture forbids. It should not be used even as an incidental utility dependency because its API makes Rust-side Neovim operations natural and tightly couples releases to Neovim internals. Recent Neovim 0.12 issues also illustrate the risk: mismatched generated option/keyset layouts caused validation failures and process-aborting panics. [nvim-oxi repository](https://github.com/noib3/nvim-oxi) [nvim-oxi design](https://crates.io/crates/nvim-oxi) [Neovim 0.12 layout issue](https://github.com/noib3/nvim-oxi/issues/311)

10. **There is no stronger maintained higher-level Lua alternative to mlua for this case.**  
    `rlua` is deprecated in favor of mlua and now serves as a transitional wrapper. Older projects such as `rust_lua_ffi` or `rust-lua` have limited activity, testing, or ecosystem adoption. Pure-Rust Lua VMs such as Piccolo embed a separate interpreter and do not bridge to Neovim’s existing Lua state, so they solve a different problem. [rlua deprecation notice](https://github.com/mlua-rs/rlua) [Piccolo repository](https://github.com/kyren/piccolo)

11. **crossbeam-channel is the best default bounded result queue.**  
    `crossbeam-channel` 0.5.16 was published on July 6, 2026 and provides MPMC bounded channels, nonblocking `try_send`/`try_recv`, iteration, and selection without requiring an async runtime. This matches an mlua architecture where Rust worker threads produce results and Lua periodically invokes a synchronous, nonblocking drain function. [crossbeam-channel crate](https://crates.io/crates/crossbeam-channel) [crossbeam-channel documentation](https://docs.rs/crossbeam-channel/latest/crossbeam_channel/)

    Suggested use:

    - `bounded(N)` for completed scan batches and watcher notices.
    - Workers use `try_send`, not blocking `send`, where shutdown could otherwise deadlock.
    - On overflow, coalesce watcher events into a single `NeedsRescan(root)` marker.
    - Lua drains at most a configured item/count/time budget per timer tick.
    - Keep a separate cancellation flag or command queue rather than placing cancellation behind a full result queue.

12. **flume is a good alternative if async interoperability becomes important.**  
    `flume` 0.12.0 was released in December 2025 and its repository was active in April 2026. It offers bounded MPMC channels plus optional async `Sink`/`Stream` and Tokio/async integration. Choose it if the Rust core already has async tasks or may be reused in a sidecar; otherwise crossbeam-channel is simpler for fred.nvim’s polling boundary. Relevant optional features include `async`, `select`, and `spin`. [flume repository](https://github.com/zesterer/flume) [flume documentation](https://docs.rs/flume/latest/flume/)

13. **notify is the maintained cross-platform filesystem watcher choice.**  
    `notify` supports inotify on Linux, FSEvents or kqueue on macOS, `ReadDirectoryChangesW` on Windows, and a polling fallback. The repository was pushed in June 2026 and is actively transitioning from v8 to v9. Optional features include:

    - `macos_fsevent`, the default macOS backend.
    - `macos_kqueue`, an alternative backend.
    - `serde`, for serializable events.
    - `serialization-compat-6`, only for old serialization compatibility.

    `notify-debouncer-mini` is convenient for basic coalescing; `notify-debouncer-full` is preferable if rename tracking, file IDs, ordering, or more complete event normalization matters. [notify repository](https://github.com/notify-rs/notify) [notify documentation](https://docs.rs/notify/latest/notify/) [mini debouncer](https://docs.rs/notify-debouncer-mini/latest/notify_debouncer_mini/) [full debouncer](https://docs.rs/notify-debouncer-full/latest/notify_debouncer_full/)

14. **Watcher events must be treated as hints, not a perfect incremental database.**  
    `notify` documents that editor save strategies vary, network filesystems may not emit events, Linux watch limits may be exhausted, and large trees can lose events. fred.nvim should therefore debounce events, normalize paths, and support a full or scoped rescan after overflow, ambiguous renames, watcher errors, or `need_rescan` indications. `PollWatcher` is a fallback for NFS, WSL-hosted paths, pseudo-filesystems, and unavailable native backends. [notify known problems](https://docs.rs/notify/latest/notify/#known-problems)

15. **ignore is the best scanner for a project/file picker.**  
    The `ignore` crate, maintained in the ripgrep repository, was released as 0.4.26 in June 2026. Its `WalkBuilder` supports `.gitignore`, `.ignore`, `.git/info/exclude`, global Git ignore rules, hidden-file controls, glob overrides, file-type matching, and parallel traversal. That is generally more useful for fred.nvim than a plain directory walker. [ignore source](https://github.com/BurntSushi/ripgrep/tree/master/crates/ignore) [ignore documentation](https://docs.rs/ignore/latest/ignore/) [WalkBuilder](https://docs.rs/ignore/latest/ignore/struct.WalkBuilder.html)

    `walkdir` remains a mature lightweight option when ignore semantics and parallelism are unnecessary, but its latest release is from March 2024. `jwalk` provides sorted parallel streaming, but its latest release is from 2022, making `ignore` the stronger maintained choice for new development. [walkdir](https://crates.io/crates/walkdir) [jwalk](https://crates.io/crates/jwalk)

## Comparison

| Option | Maintenance as of 2026-07 | Constraint fit | Main advantages | Main limitations |
|---|---|---:|---|---|
| **mlua loadable module** | Excellent; 0.12 released 2026-07 | **Strong** when domain-only | Safe conversions, userdata, Lua errors, normal `require`, low IPC overhead | Native packaging, host Lua ABI, synchronous calls block Nvim |
| **Raw LuaJIT FFI/C ABI** | LuaJIT maintained; helper sys crates mixed/stale | **Strong** if ABI is domain-only | Tiny explicit boundary, no Lua C-stack manipulation, potentially lowest overhead | Unsafe ownership, panic/ABI discipline, LuaJIT-only |
| **nvim-rs sidecar** | Maintained but modest release cadence; API marked unstable | **Weak by default** | Async RPC, process isolation, Neovim client support | Primary purpose is Rust calling Neovim APIs; serialization and lifecycle costs |
| **Custom stdio sidecar** | Built from mainstream Serde/Tokio crates | **Very strong** | Mechanical separation from Neovim API, crash isolation, no Lua ABI | More protocol/process code and IPC latency |
| **nvim-oxi** | Active in 2026 | **Fails constraint** | Typed in-process Neovim API, callback parity | Rust directly calls/hooks Neovim; version/layout coupling |
| **rlua / older binding generators** | Deprecated or stale | Poor | Familiar APIs in older code | Superseded by mlua or insufficient maintenance |

## Recommendation for fred.nvim

Use this stack:

- **Binding:** `mlua = 0.12` with `["luajit", "module", "macros"]`.
- **Queue:** `crossbeam-channel::bounded`.
- **Initial scan:** `ignore::WalkBuilder`, using its parallel walker where beneficial.
- **Watching:** current stable `notify`, optionally `notify-debouncer-mini`; use the full debouncer only if rename fidelity becomes a product requirement.
- **Cancellation:** an atomic cancellation token or a small command channel.
- **UI wakeup:** Lua-owned `vim.uv` timer, with `vim.schedule_wrap` before draining and applying results.
- **Fallback:** consider a custom stdio sidecar later if native crashes, ABI distribution, or restartability become significant operational problems.

Suggested native API shape:

```lua
local native = require("fred_native")

local task = native.start_scan({
  root = root,
  hidden = false,
  follow_links = false,
  queue_capacity = 256,
})

timer:start(0, 16, vim.schedule_wrap(function()
  local results, status = native.poll(task, 128)

  -- Lua alone updates picker buffers, extmarks, windows and notifications.
  apply_results(results)

  if status.done then
    timer:stop()
    timer:close()
  end
end))
```

Important invariants:

1. Rust never imports, stores, or calls `vim`, `vim.api`, `vim.uv`, or Neovim C symbols.
2. No `mlua::Lua`, table, function, userdata reference, or borrowed Lua string enters a worker thread.
3. Every result crossing threads owns its strings and paths.
4. Lua polls; Rust never invokes a Lua callback asynchronously.
5. Native exports return quickly; scans and watcher processing run off-thread.
6. Queue overflow produces coalesced state such as `NeedsRescan`, not unbounded memory use.
7. Shutdown is idempotent, nonblocking from Lua, and eventually joins or detaches all Rust workers.
8. Packaging declares whether LuaJIT is mandatory and validates the host environment before loading the binary.

## Sources

### Kept

- [mlua repository](https://github.com/mlua-rs/mlua) — primary feature, module-mode, platform-testing, MSRV, and maintenance evidence.
- [mlua documentation](https://docs.rs/mlua/latest/mlua/) — conversion, userdata, async, `Send`, and module API details.
- [mlua-sys](https://crates.io/crates/mlua-sys) — maintained low-level Lua/LuaJIT FFI comparison.
- [Neovim Lua documentation](https://neovim.io/doc/user/lua/) — authoritative Lua version, module loading, LuaJIT, threading, libuv, and scheduling rules.
- [Neovim API documentation](https://neovim.io/doc/user/api/) — authoritative main-loop and RPC behavior.
- [LuaJIT FFI documentation](https://github.com/LuaJIT/LuaJIT/blob/v2.1/doc/ext_ffi.html) — authoritative direct C ABI option.
- [nvim-rs repository](https://github.com/KillTheMule/nvim-rs) — official status, runtime features, tests, and maintenance evidence.
- [nvim-oxi repository](https://github.com/noib3/nvim-oxi) — credible higher-level alternative and its direct-FFI design.
- [crossbeam-channel](https://crates.io/crates/crossbeam-channel) — current bounded-channel release and adoption evidence.
- [flume repository](https://github.com/zesterer/flume) — maintained async-capable bounded-channel alternative.
- [notify repository](https://github.com/notify-rs/notify) — watcher backends, debouncer ecosystem, migration, and maintenance evidence.
- [ignore crate](https://github.com/BurntSushi/ripgrep/tree/master/crates/ignore) — maintained project-aware parallel filesystem traversal.
- [cbindgen repository](https://github.com/mozilla/cbindgen) — official Rust-to-C header generation option and stated maintenance limitations.

### Dropped

- `lua-jit-sys` — targets LuaJIT 2.0.5 and has not been released since 2017.
- `luajit2-sys` — latest release is from 2020 and embeds old beta-version constants.
- `rlua` — officially deprecated in favor of mlua.
- `rust_lua_ffi` and `rust-lua` — insufficient recent maintenance/testing compared with mlua.
- `jwalk` — useful design, but its last release is from 2022.
- SEO comparison pages and third-party generated summaries — excluded in favor of official repositories, crates.io, docs.rs, and Neovim/LuaJIT documentation.

## Gaps

- No fred.nvim-specific performance benchmark was available. A prototype should measure scan throughput, Lua conversion cost, result-drain frame time, and queue-overflow behavior on representative repositories.
- Host compatibility should be tested against fred.nvim’s actual minimum and maximum Neovim versions, including macOS, Linux, Windows, x86-64, and ARM64.
- Whether a `lua51,module` artifact can broaden support without separate per-runtime builds needs direct loading tests; native Lua ABI compatibility should not be inferred solely from Lua language compatibility.
- The exact stable `notify` major version should be pinned when implementation begins because the project is actively migrating from v8 to v9.
- No repository files, builds, or tests were changed or executed because this was a research-only task.

content.text:
有，**`mlua` 基本就是这个用途最合适、最方便的库**。

它支持 Lua 5.1/LuaJIT、可编译为能被 `require()` 加载的 `cdylib`，也支持 table、userdata、错误和 Rust 类型转换。[官方文档明确提供了 module mode](https://github.com/mlua-rs/mlua/blob/8eddf85de0e1576f07145d04201d07ca108d963c/README.md#L157-L184)，`#[mlua::lua_module]` 会直接生成 `luaopen_xxx` 入口。[源码说明](https://github.com/mlua-rs/mlua/blob/8eddf85de0e1576f07145d04201d07ca108d963c/docs/lua_module.md#L1-L13)

## 推荐架构

你的架构方向是对的，但要明确分成三层：

```text
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ fred-core                                   │
│ 纯 Rust、编辑器无关                         │
│                                             │
│ path / NodeId / snapshot / scanner          │
│ watcher / intent / planner / executor       │
│ task / cancellation / semantic frame        │
│                                             │
│ 不依赖：mlua / nvim-oxi / Neovim / UI       │
└─────────────────────┬───────────────────────┘
                      │ Rust API
┌─────────────────────▼───────────────────────┐
│ fred-lua                                    │
│ 使用 mlua 的薄 binding crate                │
│                                             │
│ Lua table/userdata ↔ fred-core 类型          │
│ start_task / poll_events / cancel / destroy │
│ 不调用任何 vim.api                          │
└─────────────────────┬───────────────────────┘
                      │ require("fred_native")
┌─────────────────────▼───────────────────────┐
│ lua/fred                                    │
│ Neovim presentation / projection adapter    │
│                                             │
│ buffer / extmark / windows / autocmd        │
│ vim.uv / cursor / undo / preview / keymaps  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

这里要区分：

- `fred-lua` 是 **语言绑定层**；
- `lua/fred` 才是 **Neovim 渲染和交互层**；
- `fred-core` 只输出 projection-neutral 的 semantic frame 和 operation result。

未来适配其他宿主时：

```text
VSCode  -> fred-napi / napi-rs -> fred-core
Emacs   -> Emacs module 或 RPC -> fred-core
CLI     -> fred-cli            -> fred-core
Neovim  -> fred-lua            -> fred-core
```

不需要现在就设计公共 `Renderer` trait。只要依赖方向固定为：

```text
fred-lua ──────> fred-core
fred-core ──X──> mlua / nvim-oxi / Neovim
```

以后添加第二个 adapter 就很容易。

## 建议使用的库

### 1. Lua ↔ Rust：`mlua`

推荐配置大致是：

```toml
[lib]
crate-type = ["cdylib"]

[dependencies]
fred-core = { path = "../fred-core" }
mlua = {
  version = "0.12",
  features = ["luajit", "module", "macros"]
}
```

`mlua` 官方支持 LuaJIT 和 native module mode，并在 Windows、macOS、Linux 上测试 module 构建。[功能列表](https://github.com/mlua-rs/mlua/blob/8eddf85de0e1576f07145d04201d07ca108d963c/README.md#L23-L51)

注意：

- 不启用 `vendored`，要使用 Neovim 自己的 Lua runtime；
- 暂时不启用 `send`；
- `mlua::Lua`、Lua table/function/string 引用绝不能进入 worker thread；
- worker thread 之间只传 owned Rust DTO；
- 初期明确要求 LuaJIT，其他 Lua runtime 后续再做兼容性测试。

### 2. 后台结果队列：`crossbeam-channel`

它提供 bounded MPMC channel、非阻塞接收和 select，不要求 Tokio，很适合：

```text
Rust worker
  -> bounded result queue
  -> Lua 调用 poll_events(max_items)
```

[`crossbeam-channel` 是 Crossbeam 官方的消息传递组件](https://github.com/crossbeam-rs/crossbeam/blob/b23b7e8eca2efdad9bdc1ceb1aee1207a852c03b/README.md#L25-L34)。

推荐规则：

- 普通 progress event 可以合并；
- watcher event 溢出时变成一个 `NeedsRescan`；
- terminal result 不允许被静默丢弃；
- cancellation 使用独立 `AtomicBool`，不能排在已满的结果队列后面；
- Lua 每次最多按数量或时间预算消费一批。

`flume` 也可以，但它的主要优势是 async `Sink/Stream`。当前设计不使用 Tokio，因此 `crossbeam-channel` 更简单。

### 3. 文件监听：`notify` + `notify-debouncer-mini`

`notify` 覆盖：

- Linux：inotify；
- macOS：FSEvents/kqueue；
- Windows：ReadDirectoryChangesW。

见其[平台后端说明](https://github.com/notify-rs/notify/blob/bc257049798e17029051eed24bcf5ae8a0f8cb85/README.md#L47-L49)。

推荐使用 `notify-debouncer-mini`，而不是 full：

- FRED 把 watcher 当提示；
- FRED 不通过 file ID 推断外部 rename identity；
- full debouncer 的 rename stitching/file-ID tracking 对当前模型没有必要。

### 4. Ignore 规则：`ignore` crate 的 matcher

`ignore` crate 很适合实现 gitignore-like 语法和 `!` negation。它提供 `GitignoreBuilder` 和 override matcher。[相关实现](https://github.com/BurntSushi/ripgrep/blob/227381db0ee83dfa4341f1e27ff9617c0f5ad992/crates/ignore/src/gitignore.rs#L78-L95)

但我不建议直接把 `WalkBuilder` 当作 FRED 的完整 scanner，因为 FRED 还需要：

- selective coverage；
- 每目录 Complete/Partial/Failed；
- NodeId 复用；
- scan generations；
- 精确取消；
- retained-unverified 节点；
- 多 View coverage union。

因此应当：

```text
ignore matcher：使用 ignore crate
directory traversal：FRED 自己基于 read_dir 实现
```

### 5. 其他小型辅助库

可以考虑：

- `thiserror`：领域错误类型；
- `serde`：简单 request/event DTO，但 native path 不应无脑转 UTF-8；
- `slotmap` 或 `slab`：TaskHandle、ViewHandle、SessionHandle registry；
- `tempfile`：planner/executor 测试；
- `proptest`：snapshot/path/planner property tests。

## `nvim-oxi` 还能不能保留？

可以，但只能这样：

```text
fred-core
  不依赖 nvim-oxi

fred-nvim-bridge
  依赖 nvim-oxi
  只负责 module registration、类型转换和 wakeup
```

也就是说，**可移植性并不要求完全禁止 `nvim-oxi`，而是要求它永远进不了 core**。

不过，如果 `nvim-oxi` 只是为了：

- 导出 Lua module；
- table/userdata 转换；
- Lua 调 Rust；

那么 `mlua` 已经足够，而且更窄、更不容易让 Rust 不小心调用 Neovim API。

只有当我们非常需要 `nvim-oxi::libuv::AsyncHandle` 主动唤醒 Neovim 时，保留它才有明显价值。但这样 Neovim adapter 仍然需要按 Neovim 版本做兼容，而 `mlua + Lua-owned timer polling` 可以避免这层耦合。

## 推荐结论

建议最终采用：

```text
fred-core
  + crossbeam-channel
  + notify
  + ignore matcher

fred-lua
  + mlua

lua/fred
  + vim.api
  + vim.uv timer
```

Rust 暴露的接口保持类似：

```text
start_scan(request) -> TaskHandle
poll_events(handle, max_items) -> events
cancel(handle)
release(handle)

apply_prepare(view, capture) -> PreparedApply
apply_finish(prepared, request) -> TaskHandle
```

Rust 永远不会：

- 调用 `vim.api`；
-访问 buffer/window/extmark；
- 在后台线程调用 Lua callback；
- 保存 Lua table/function/string 引用；
- 知道当前宿主是 Neovim、VSCode 还是 Emacs。

**是否同意按 `fred-core + mlua binding + Neovim Lua renderer` 这个三层结构修订 design，并把 `nvim-oxi` 从必需依赖改为不采用的备选 adapter？**
ok

content.text:
好的。下一节确定 **任务与通信模型**。

## 设计第 2 节：Engine、任务与事件传输

### 1. 一个宿主进程只有一个 Core Engine

`fred-core` 提供普通 Rust 对象：

```text
Engine
  RootSessions
  Views
  Tasks
  MutationCoordinator
  active mutation fence
  watcher state
  event mailbox
```

Neovim Lua adapter 创建一个 `Engine`。因此“一个 Neovim 进程只能执行一个 FRED mutation”实际表达为：

> 一个 Engine 同时最多执行一个 mutation。

不使用 Rust 全局静态单例，这样单元测试和未来的 VSCode、Emacs adapter 可以独立创建 Engine。

### 2. Native 调用必须快速返回

只有纯内存、耗时有明确上限的调用可以同步完成，例如：

```text
engine:create_session()
engine:create_view()
engine:commit_projection()
engine:allocate_node_ids()
engine:cancel(task)
```

可能涉及 I/O 或大量计算的操作全部返回 `TaskHandle`：

```text
engine:start_scan(request)           -> TaskHandle
engine:start_metadata(request)       -> TaskHandle
engine:start_apply_prepare(request)  -> TaskHandle
engine:start_apply_finish(prepared)  -> TaskHandle
engine:start_refresh(request)        -> TaskHandle
```

因此 Rust 不会长时间阻塞 Neovim 主线程。

### 3. 一个 Engine 级别的 mailbox

不为每个 task 创建 Lua timer。Lua 维护一个进程级 drain loop：

```lua
local events = native_engine:poll({
  max_events = 128,
  max_frame_nodes = 500,
})
```

`poll()`：

- 永不阻塞；
- 只返回 owned DTO；
- 每次有数量和数据量上限；
- 所有 Lua table/userdata 都只在调用 `poll()` 的主线程创建；
- worker thread 永远不持有 `mlua::Lua` 或 Lua value。

事件大致包括：

```text
ScanProgress
FrameBegin
FrameNodeBatch
FrameDirectoryBatch
FrameComplete
MetadataBatch
WatcherNeedsRescan
ApplyProgress
ApplyPrepared
ApplyFinished
TaskFailed
TaskCancelled
```

所有事件携带对应的 task、session、view 和 generation identity。

### 4. Terminal result 不能放在可能溢出的普通队列里

mailbox 分为两类状态：

```text
Transient mailbox
  bounded
  progress 可以覆盖或合并
  watcher path 可以合并为 NeedsRescan

Terminal result store
  每个 task 最多一个
  保留到 Lua 成功消费
  不受 transient queue 容量影响
```

规则：

- progress queue 满时允许丢弃旧 progress；
- 多个 watcher event 合并成一个 scoped `NeedsRescan`；
- `ApplyFinished`、`TaskFailed`、完整 frame 等 terminal result 不能丢失；
- terminal result 只能消费一次；
- task handle 销毁前，尚未消费的 terminal result 仍由 Engine 持有。

### 5. Lua 使用自适应的单一 timer

Lua 层负责 `vim.uv`：

```text
有活跃 scan/apply/render：
  高频 drain，例如 10–16ms

只有 watcher：
  低频 drain，例如 100–250ms

没有 session、task 或 watcher：
  timer 停止
```

具体间隔属于 Lua presentation 策略，不进入 `fred-core`。

在 `BufWriteCmd` 等必须等待 terminal result 的路径中，Lua 复用相同的 drain 函数，并由 `ApplyBufferGuard` 防止重入。

### 6. Cancellation 和 shutdown

Cancellation 不通过可能已满的 result queue：

```text
TaskState
  cancellation: AtomicBool
  terminal_result
```

规则：

- `cancel(task)` 是快速、幂等的；
- worker 在安全检查点读取 `AtomicBool`；
- task panic 在 Rust 边界捕获并转成 terminal error；
- `engine:shutdown()` 幂等；
- Lua buffer 销毁不会要求 Rust worker 调用 Lua；
- 最后一个 owner 释放后，task/session 按普通 Rust ownership 退出。

### 7. `PreparedApply` 的归属

Core 中是普通的一次性 capability：

```text
PreparedApplyCore {
  Fresh | Consumed
  immutable ValidPlan
  authority stamp
}
```

`fred-lua` 将它包装为 mlua userdata：

```text
Lua userdata
  └── owns PreparedApplyCore
```

- preview cancel：Lua 丢弃 userdata，Rust `Drop` 释放 plan；
- execute：adapter 原子消费 capability 并创建 finish task；
- duplicate/replay：core 拒绝；
- capability 不包含 Neovim buffer/window 信息。

这能保留原设计中的 sealed one-shot 语义，同时让 core 完全不知道 Lua 和 Neovim。

**这一节的 Engine 级 mailbox、非阻塞 polling 和 terminal-result-store 模型是否认可？**

summary:
## Goal
- Review `2026-07-15-fred-design.md` for architectural/specification issues and determine the next development step.
- Redefine the Rust/Lua boundary so Lua owns all Neovim integration while Rust provides filesystem/domain capabilities.
- Identify convenient, maintained libraries for connecting a Rust core to Lua inside Neovim.

## Constraints & Preferences
- Follow the brainstorming hard gate: do not implement, write code, or scaffold until the revised design is approved.
- Lua must own every Neovim API, buffer, UI, scheduling, and event-loop interaction.
- Rust should primarily own filesystem/domain logic; `nvim-oxi` is optional and should not be a hard dependency.
- After the written spec is approved, the only next skill is `writing-plans`.
- Prefer milestone-specific plans rather than one monolithic implementation plan.

## Progress
### Done
- [x] Located and reviewed `/Users/bingcoke/project/lua/fred.nvim/2026-07-15-fred-design.md` (2500 lines, approximately 220 KB).
- [x] Inspected repository state and recent commits; no implementation files or existing `plan.md`/`progress.md` were found.
- [x] Completed two independent read-only specification reviews.
- [x] Verified relevant Neovim 0.12.4 and `nvim-oxi` assumptions against upstream documentation/source.
- [x] Identified that mandatory `nvim-oxi`, exact Neovim patch/revision pairing, Rust-side `AsyncHandle`, and native Neovim registration contradict the clarified architecture.
- [x] Identified additional blockers: scan/mutation snapshot publication race, private buffer URI lifecycle, coverage ownership, incomplete filename escape grammar, Windows symlink-kind semantics, missing fault-injection seams, underspecified `reveal()`, and oversized v1 scope.
- [x] Presented three tentative bridge approaches: Lua-loadable native module with polling, pipe/event notification, and sidecar RPC. Recommended polling as the simplest option, but the user has not approved it.
- [x] No project files were modified.

### In Progress
- [ ] Researching maintained Rust↔Lua/Neovim bridge libraries, including `mlua`, LuaJIT FFI/C ABI approaches, `nvim-rs`/sidecar designs, bounded queues, filesystem watchers, and scanners.
- [ ] Async researcher run: `7dccfc77-5bb7-490f-862a-f4d1dc7f092a`.

### Blocked
- The design cannot proceed to an implementation plan until the Rust/Lua bridge and async result-delivery protocol are selected.
- The spec still embeds obsolete `nvim-oxi` and exact Neovim-patch artifact assumptions.
- The revised design must resolve the other architectural ambiguities before approval.

## Key Decisions
- **Lua owns Neovim integration**: All Neovim API calls, buffers, extmarks, windows, autocmds, timers, scheduling, and UI remain in Lua.
- **Rust is Neovim-agnostic**: Rust should expose filesystem/domain operations, task handles, cancellation, bounded results, and opaque capabilities without calling Neovim APIs.
- **No mandatory `nvim-oxi` pairing**: Neovim compatibility belongs to Lua/headless tests and should not determine Rust artifact identity.
- **Milestone planning**: Keep the current document as an umbrella architecture spec, then create bounded plans, beginning with M0 for bridge/loading/task delivery and Neovim compatibility experiments.
- **Bridge choice remains open**: The polling-based Lua-loadable module was recommended, but the user explicitly requested investigation of more convenient libraries before deciding.

## Next Steps
1. Retrieve the researcher result with `subagent(action="wait", id="7dccfc77-5bb7-490f-862a-f4d1dc7f092a")`.
2. Compare credible libraries by LuaJIT compatibility, `require()` support, maintenance, cross-platform packaging, thread/result delivery, and Neovim-version coupling.
3. Present 2–3 concrete bridge choices with trade-offs and a recommendation; ask one decision question.
4. Revise `2026-07-15-fred-design.md` after the user selects the bridge.
5. Resolve the remaining architecture blockers and split acceptance criteria by milestone.
6. Self-review and commit the revised spec, then ask the user to review it.
7. After approval, invoke `writing-plans` for M0 only.

## Critical Context
- Project root: `/Users/bingcoke/project/lua/fred.nvim`
- Design file: `/Users/bingcoke/project/lua/fred.nvim/2026-07-15-fred-design.md`
- Neovim 0.12.4 documents `BufWriteCmd` with: `"The buffer contents should not be changed."` This must be validated as an M0 architecture risk because the design expects synchronous terminal projection rendering during write handling.
- Main obsolete design references include lines `15`, `194-248`, `2081-2106`, `2378-2404`, and `2412`.
- Research task specifically asks for official evidence on `mlua`, low-level LuaJIT FFI/C ABI, `nvim-rs`/remote sidecars, higher-level alternatives, bounded cross-thread queues, filesystem watching, and scanning.

---

**Turn Context (split turn):**

## Original Request
用户要求：若没有更合适的替代方案，可以继续使用 `nvim-oxi`，但必须明确它只承担 Lua ↔ Rust 的通信/适配职责。Rust 核心库不能感知 Neovim，整体设计应与编辑器无关，未来能轻松接入 Emacs、VSCode 等；Lua/Neovim 只是多个渲染层或 presentation adapter 之一。

## Early Progress
- 已确认采用严格分层：
  - Rust 核心库：领域逻辑、状态、索引、事件等，完全不依赖 Neovim/Lua。
  - `nvim-oxi`：仅放在最薄的 Neovim adapter/transport crate 中。
  - Lua：负责调用桥接及 Neovim UI 投影，不属于领域核心。
- 调查了替代通信方案：
  - `mlua` 支持 LuaJIT，并可通过 `cdylib`、`module` feature 和 `#[mlua::lua_module]` 构建可被 Neovim `require` 的原生 Lua 模块，因此可作为不依赖 `nvim-oxi` 的 Lua ↔ Rust 桥接方案。
  - `nvim-rs` 是 Neovim msgpack-RPC 客户端，仍属 Neovim 专用边界，且 README 标注 API 不稳定，不适合进入核心库。
- 开始调查核心层可使用的通用库：
  - `crossbeam-channel`：有界队列、跨线程事件通信及背压。
  - `notify` / debouncer：跨平台文件系统监听与事件去抖。
  - `ignore`：遵循 `.gitignore` 等规则的目录遍历，可并行扫描。
  - `flume`：仓库地址最初写错，正确仓库是 `zesterer/flume`。

## Context for Suffix
- 后续应围绕“编辑器无关核心 + 每个编辑器独立 adapter/renderer”给出最终 crate 分层和依赖方向。
- 无论最终选 `nvim-oxi` 还是 `mlua`，它们都只能出现在 Neovim/Lua 边界层，不能泄漏 `Buffer`、`Window`、Lua value 或 Neovim API 类型到核心。
- 需要比较 `nvim-oxi` 与 `mlua` 的桥接成本，并结合 channel、文件监听、ignore 扫描等库确定实现方案；Emacs/VSCode 应能通过新增 adapter 消费同一套核心命令、事件和渲染模型。

content.text:
## 设计第 3 节：Core 数据契约与文本投影边界

为了让 VSCode/Emacs 不必模拟 Neovim buffer，建议将“领域模型”和“可编辑文本格式”再分开一层。

```text
fred-core
  editor-neutral domain

fred-text
  optional editable-text codec
  depends on fred-core

fred-lua
  mlua binding
  depends on fred-core + fred-text

lua/fred
  Neovim buffer/UI renderer
```

依赖方向：

```text
fred-text ──> fred-core
fred-lua  ──> fred-text + fred-core
fred-core ─X─> Lua / Neovim / text buffer
```

### 1. `fred-core` 拥有的模型

Core 拥有：

```text
Engine
RootSession
Snapshot
NodeId / NodeRecord
ViewState
Coverage
Task
Intent
ValidPlan
PreparedApply
OperationResult
WatcherHint
```

其中 `ViewState` 只包含领域状态：

- expanded node set；
- filter/sort policy；
- hidden-file policy；
- coverage request；
- generation；
- semantic projection。

它不包含：

- `bufnr`；
- `winid`；
- cursor row；
- extmark ID；
- highlight namespace；
- timer；
- autocmd；
- Lua callback。

Lua 使用 opaque handles 将 Neovim runtime 与 Core view 关联：

```text
Lua BufferRuntime
  bufnr
  extmark namespace
  timer
  cursor state
  render generation
  core ViewHandle
```

### 2. Core 输出 semantic projection，不输出 Neovim 行

Core 可以输出：

```text
ProjectionEntry {
  node_id,
  parent_id,
  depth,
  kind,
  expansion_state,
  coverage_state,
  pending_operation,
  diagnostic_state,
}
```

Core 不输出：

- icon；
- highlight group；
- extmark；
- statusline；
- float contents；
- Neovim-specific virtual text。

`fred-text` 在这些 semantic entries 之上定义一种可逆的 editable document format。Neovim Lua 将生成的 document 放入 buffer，并负责 extmark、highlight 和 window 投影。

未来：

- Emacs buffer adapter 可以复用 `fred-text`；
- VSCode TreeView 可以直接消费 `ProjectionEntry`，完全跳过 `fred-text`；
- CLI 可以选择使用 `fred-text` 或自己渲染。

暂不设计通用 `Renderer` trait；等第二个 adapter 出现后再抽象。

### 3. 编辑捕获使用通用 identity anchors

Neovim Lua 在 apply 前同步捕获：

```text
TextCapture {
  projection_generation,
  rows: [
    CaptureRow {
      text,
      anchor: Option<NodeId>,
    }
  ]
}
```

`anchor` 来自 Lua 管理的 extmark，但 Core 不知道 extmark 是什么。

含义：

- 原有节点行带 `NodeId`；
- 用户新插入的行没有 `NodeId`；
- 被删除的 anchor 表示候选删除；
- 节点移动通过同一 `NodeId` 出现在不同父级位置识别；
- 文本变化加相同 `NodeId` 表示候选 rename。

其他 adapter 可以用自己的稳定 identity 机制生成相同的 `TextCapture`，不必使用 extmark。

### 4. `fred-text` 只负责可编辑文本协议

`fred-text` 负责：

```text
semantic projection -> editable text document
captured document    -> IntentDraft
```

然后：

```text
IntentDraft
  -> fred-core validation/probes
  -> ValidPlan
  -> PreparedApply
```

也就是说：

- 文本语法错误由 `fred-text` 报告；
- filesystem 冲突和权限错误由 `fred-core` 报告；
- Lua 只负责把 diagnostic 显示到对应行；
- Lua 不自行推断 rename/move/delete。

VSCode 等结构化 UI 可以直接构造 `IntentDraft`，绕过文本解析。

### 5. Native filename 必须保持无损

Core 内部始终使用：

```text
PathBuf
OsString
NativeName
```

不能把 filesystem identity 建立在 UTF-8 字符串上。

`fred-text` 定义完整的可逆名称编码：

#### Unix

文件名按原始 bytes 处理：

```text
\\      literal backslash
\t      tab
\n      newline
\r      carriage return
\xNN    arbitrary byte/control/non-UTF-8 byte
```

例如文件名的原始 bytes：

```text
66 6f 80 6f
```

显示为：

```text
fo\x80o
```

名称中本来存在的字面量 `\x80` 会编码为：

```text
\\x80
```

因此不会产生歧义。

#### Windows

Windows 名称按 UTF-16 code units 处理：

- 正常 Unicode 显示为 UTF-8；
- 控制字符使用标准 escape；
- 未配对 surrogate 使用 `\u{NNNN}`；
- parser 必须恢复原始 UTF-16 units。

解析规则是严格的：

- 不认识的 escape 报语法错误；
- 截断的 `\x`/`\u{}` 报语法错误；
- 不做 replacement-character lossy conversion；
- 不隐式 trim leading/trailing spaces。

### 6. 路径组件而不是完整路径参与编辑

文本行表示一个 filename component，不允许用户通过名称字段偷偷注入路径：

```text
Unix:
  "/" 不可能是合法 component

Windows:
  "/"、"\"、drive prefix、UNC prefix 不允许作为 component
```

移动操作由 tree parent identity 表达，不通过解析用户输入的完整字符串路径表达。

最终领域 intent 类似：

```text
Rename {
  node: NodeId,
  new_name: NativeName,
}

Move {
  node: NodeId,
  new_parent: NodeId,
  new_name: NativeName,
}

Create {
  parent: NodeId,
  name: NativeName,
  kind: NodeKind,
}

Delete {
  node: NodeId,
}
```

这样可以避免字符串路径拼接导致的 traversal、separator 和平台差异问题。

### 7. Diagnostic 也是 editor-neutral

Core/text codec 返回：

```text
Diagnostic {
  severity,
  code,
  message,
  capture_row: Option<RowIndex>,
  node_id: Option<NodeId>,
  related_nodes,
  retryable,
}
```

不返回 Neovim highlight 或 notification 参数。

Lua 决定：

- 行内 highlight；
- virtual text；
- float；
- `vim.notify`；
- cursor jump。

这一边界可以保证 Rust domain、editable text codec 和 Neovim presentation 三者不会互相泄漏实现细节。

**这一节的 `fred-core` / `fred-text` / `fred-lua` 分层，以及无损 filename grammar 是否认可？**
fred-text 是rust的模块吗?

content.text:
对，`fred-text` 是 **Rust workspace 中的独立 library crate**，不是 Lua 模块。更准确的名字可以叫 `fred-text-codec`，避免被理解为 UI renderer。

建议目录结构：

```text
crates/
  fred-core/
    src/
      engine.rs
      snapshot.rs
      intent.rs
      planner.rs
      executor.rs

  fred-text/
    src/
      encode.rs       # NativeName 无损编码
      decode.rs
      render.rs       # semantic projection -> 文本文档
      parse.rs        # TextCapture -> IntentDraft
      diagnostic.rs

  fred-lua/
    src/
      lib.rs          # mlua module
      conversion.rs   # Rust DTO <-> Lua table/userdata

lua/
  fred/
    buffer.lua
    render.lua
    apply.lua
    ui.lua
```

依赖关系：

```text
fred-text ──> fred-core
fred-lua  ──> fred-core + fred-text
Lua       ──> fred-lua
```

`fred-text` 只做一种 **editor-neutral codec**：

```text
ProjectionEntry
  -> canonical editable text

TextCapture
  -> IntentDraft
```

它不会：

- 创建或修改 Neovim buffer；
- 调用 `vim.api`；
- 管理 extmark/highlight/window；
- 包含 `mlua` 类型；
- 执行 filesystem mutation。

因此 Lua 仍然是 Neovim renderer。可以把它类比为：

```text
fred-text 定义 HTML/JSON 格式
Lua 决定把格式放进哪个窗口、怎样高亮和交互
```

VSCode TreeView 可以直接消费 `fred-core::ProjectionEntry`，完全不依赖 `fred-text`；而 Emacs 的 editable buffer adapter 可以选择复用它。

我推荐将其作为独立 Rust crate，并在文档中称为 **optional editable-text codec**，而不是 rendering layer。
rust应该就是只有文件模型什么的, 还有计划器执行器, 就是上层感知变化,  为什么会有fred_test的模块?

content.text:
你说得对。我刚才把 editable-text protocol 抽成 `fred-text` 是**过度设计**，而且违背了你定义的边界。应该删除这个模块。

## 修正后的职责

### Rust：只负责文件领域

```text
fred-core
  filesystem model
  snapshots / NodeId
  scanning / metadata
  coverage subscriptions
  filesystem watching
  intent validation
  planner
  executor
  rollback/recovery
  task/cancellation
```

Rust 不应该知道：

- 文本行；
- indentation；
- filename 如何显示或 escape；
- extmark；
- cursor；
- 哪一行发生了变化；
- Neovim view；
- 用户是通过编辑 buffer、TreeView 还是 GUI 发起操作。

Core 中也不需要持久化 UI `ViewState`。最多维护一个编辑器无关的 `CoverageLease`/`Subscription`，表示某个消费者当前要求加载哪些目录。

### `fred-lua`：极薄的 Rust binding

这是唯一额外的 Rust crate：

```text
fred-lua
  mlua module registration
  Lua DTO ↔ fred-core DTO
  opaque handle wrapping
  poll/cancel/destroy
```

它不包含文本 parser、renderer 或 Neovim API。

### Lua：感知和解释用户变化

```text
lua/fred
  expansion/filter/sort state
  semantic model -> buffer lines
  filename display escaping
  extmark identity tracking
  compare original projection with edited buffer
  edited buffer -> semantic intents
  diagnostics -> row/highlight
  all Neovim APIs
```

## 正确的数据流

```text
Rust Core
  文件模型 / snapshot
        │
        ▼
Lua
  渲染为 Neovim buffer
  建立 NodeId extmarks
        │
        ▼
用户编辑 buffer
        │
        ▼
Lua
  根据文本和 extmark 感知：
  rename / move / create / delete
        │
        ▼
Rust Core
  接收 semantic intents
  validate -> plan -> execute
        │
        ▼
Rust Core
  返回 OperationResult / SnapshotDelta
        │
        ▼
Lua
  更新并重新渲染 buffer
```

例如 Rust 接收到的已经是语义操作：

```text
Rename {
  node_id,
  new_name,
}

Move {
  node_id,
  new_parent_id,
  new_name,
}

Create {
  parent_id,
  name,
  kind,
}

Delete {
  node_id,
}
```

Rust 不接收：

```text
TextCapture
CaptureRow
row index
indentation
buffer text
extmark ID
```

## Filename 边界

Core 内部保留 `OsString`/native filename。

`fred-lua` 只做无损 transport：

```text
Unix:
  binary Lua string containing filename bytes

Windows:
  tagged binary data containing UTF-16LE units
```

具体怎样显示：

```text
\x80
\n
\t
\\
```

以及怎样把用户编辑后的字符串解析回 native name，全部由 Lua presentation 层负责。Rust 最后只验证结果是否是当前平台合法的单个 filename component。

因此最终只有：

```text
crates/
  fred-core/
  fred-lua/

lua/
  fred/
```

没有 `fred-text`。

**修正后的核心原则是：Lua 感知 UI 文本变化并产生 semantic intents，Rust 只验证、规划和执行这些 intents。**
对

content.text:
## 设计第 4 节：文件模型、Snapshot 发布与扫描一致性

这一节全部属于 `fred-core`，不涉及 Lua 文本或 Neovim。

### 1. RootSession 文件模型

每个 workspace root 对应一个 `RootSession`：

```text
RootSession {
  root capability,
  published snapshot,
  generation,
  mutation epoch,
  coverage leases,
  dirty scopes,
  active tasks,
}
```

Snapshot 是不可变对象：

```text
Snapshot {
  generation,
  nodes: NodeId -> NodeRecord,
  directory coverage,
}
```

读取方可以持有：

```text
Arc<Snapshot>
```

scanner、watcher 和 executor 都不能直接修改已经发布的 Snapshot。它们只能生成候选 observation/delta，最后由 RootSession coordinator 原子发布。

### 2. Node identity 规则

`NodeId`：

- 只在一个 RootSession 内有意义；
- 是 opaque identity，不从 path hash 推导；
- 不暴露 inode/file ID 作为公共 identity；
- 不因一次普通 refresh 改变；
- 不跨 session 复用。

身份保持规则：

| 变化来源 | NodeId 行为 |
|---|---|
| FRED 执行 rename | 保持 |
| FRED 执行 move | 保持 |
| FRED 执行内容修改 | 保持 |
| refresh 发现同一 parent/name 仍存在 | 保持 |
| 外部 delete 后重新 create | 新 NodeId |
| watcher 报告外部 rename | 不推断身份转移 |
| 外部 old-name 消失、new-name 出现 | old 删除，new 分配新 NodeId |

因此 watcher 的 rename cookie、inode 或 Windows file ID 不用来偷偷保持 NodeId。

### 3. 唯一的 Snapshot 发布入口

所有发布经过 RootSession coordinator：

```text
scanner worker
  -> ScanObservation

executor
  -> MutationDelta

watcher
  -> DirtyScope hint

RootSession coordinator
  -> validate generation
  -> construct next immutable Snapshot
  -> atomic publish
  -> generation += 1
```

worker thread 不获得可变 Snapshot 引用。

Lua 看到的只是：

```text
ModelChanged {
  root_session,
  generation,
  changed_node_ids,
  dirty_scopes,
}
```

收到事件后，Lua 可以用 `query_children()` 等接口读取新的 semantic model。

### 4. 解决 scan/mutation publication race

每个 scan task 固定记录：

```text
ScanTask {
  base_generation,
  base_mutation_epoch,
  scope,
}
```

scan 完成后，发布前检查：

```text
base_generation == current_generation
base_mutation_epoch == current_mutation_epoch
```

如果任一不匹配：

- scan 结果不发布；
- 不尝试把 stale tree 与新 Snapshot 智能 merge；
- scope 被重新标记为 dirty；
- scheduler 发起新的 scoped scan。

这可能重复少量 I/O，但能避免：

```text
1. scan 从旧 snapshot 开始
2. mutation rename A -> B 并发布
3. 旧 scan 完成
4. 旧 scan 把 A 重新发布回来
```

v1 不做复杂 rebase。正确性优先于减少重复扫描。

### 5. Mutation 发布顺序

执行流程：

```text
PreparedApply
  -> 获取 Engine mutation gate
  -> 验证 authority stamp
  -> 重新执行必要 filesystem probes
  -> mutation_epoch += 1
  -> 执行 ValidPlan
  -> 生成 MutationDelta
  -> 原子发布新 Snapshot
  -> generation += 1
  -> mutation_epoch += 1
  -> 标记 touched scopes 需要 reconciliation scan
  -> 释放 mutation gate
```

mutation 期间完成的旧 scanner observation 会因为 epoch/generation 不匹配而被拒绝。

MutationDelta 根据已成功执行的步骤更新模型；随后 scoped reconciliation scan 用真实 filesystem 状态确认结果。

如果执行部分失败：

- Snapshot 只反映已确认发生的操作；
- affected scopes 标记 dirty；
- 返回 structured partial-failure result；
- reconciliation scan 必须运行；
- 不通过恢复旧 Snapshot 来假装 filesystem 已回滚。

### 6. Coverage 使用 lease，不使用 UI View

Core 不知道 Neovim view。Lua 为每个使用者维护一个 `CoverageLease`：

```text
lease = engine:create_coverage_lease(root_session)

lease:update({
  directories = {
    { node = root, depth = 2 },
    { node = src,  depth = 1 },
  }
})

lease:release()
```

RootSession 的 desired coverage 是所有有效 lease 的 union。

用途：

- Lua 展开目录时扩大 lease；
- Lua 折叠目录时可以缩小 lease；
- picker/reveal 可以创建临时 lease；
- buffer 关闭时释放 lease；
- 不再 requested 的节点可以保留在 Snapshot cache 中，不要求立即删除；
- lease 只控制扫描、刷新和 watcher 资源，不代表节点是否存在。

Coverage 状态由 Core 明确记录：

```text
Unscanned
Partial { reason }
Complete { observed_at }
Failed { error }
Dirty { previous_coverage }
```

读取部分目录失败时不能把它标成 `Complete`。

### 7. Watcher 只产生 dirty hints

推荐：

```text
notify
  + notify-debouncer-mini
```

Watcher event 不直接修改文件模型：

```text
watcher event
  -> normalize event scope
  -> mark directory/subtree dirty
  -> coalesce duplicate scopes
  -> schedule scoped scan
```

规则：

- watcher event 不作为事实；
- watcher rename 不作为 identity 证据；
- queue overflow 转成 `NeedsRescan(scope)`；
- backend error 转成 `NeedsRescan(root)` 或 watcher diagnostic；
- NFS/WSL/不可靠 backend 可退回 `PollWatcher`；
- mutation 自己产生的 watcher event 同样允许进入 reconciliation 流程。

### 8. Symlink 和 Windows reparse point

v1 建议固定：

```text
follow_links = false
```

分类使用 `symlink_metadata`，先判断 directory entry 自身，而不是目标：

```text
regular file
directory
symlink/reparse point
special
```

规则：

- Unix symlink 不遍历；
- Windows junction/reparse point 不当作普通目录遍历；
- 可以额外记录可选的 `target_kind` 用于显示；
- `target_kind` 不改变节点自身 kind；
- broken symlink 仍然是有效 symlink node；
- 不需要在 v1 实现 symlink cycle detection。

以后如支持 follow-links，应作为新的显式策略加入，并单独设计 capability、安全边界和 cycle detection。

### 9. Lua 查询模型，而不是接收 Rust 渲染行

Core 提供语义查询，例如：

```text
query_node(NodeId)
query_children(NodeId)
query_ancestors(NodeId)
resolve_relative_path(NativePath)
snapshot_generation()
coverage_status(NodeId)
```

返回：

```text
NodeRecord {
  node_id,
  parent_id,
  native_name,
  kind,
  metadata,
  coverage,
}
```

Lua 决定排序、过滤、展开和最终文本格式。

这一模型用保守策略解决两个关键问题：

1. scan 不能覆盖较新的 mutation Snapshot；
2. watcher 不能被误当作准确的增量数据库。

**是否认可 v1 采用“stale scan 直接丢弃并重新 scoped scan”以及默认绝不跟随 symlink 的保守模型？**

. 解决 scan/mutation publication race
 是什么依稀?
是什么意思?

content.text:
这里的 **scan/mutation publication race**，简单说就是：

> **一个较早开始的扫描，在较晚的文件操作完成之后才结束，然后用旧扫描结果覆盖了新的内存文件模型。**

它不会把磁盘文件真的改回去，但会让 Rust 内存中的文件树和磁盘不一致。

## 示例

最开始磁盘和 Rust 模型都是：

```text
a.txt
```

时间顺序如下：

```text
T1：后台扫描开始
    扫描任务看到 a.txt

T2：用户通过 FRED 执行 rename
    a.txt -> b.txt

T3：planner/executor 成功完成 rename
    Rust 文件模型更新为 b.txt

T4：T1 开始的旧扫描现在才结束
    它把自己看到的 a.txt 发布为最新模型
```

结果变成：

```text
真实磁盘：b.txt
Rust 模型：a.txt    <- 错误
Lua UI：  a.txt     <- 错误
```

这里的 **publication** 就是：

> 扫描结束后，把扫描结果正式设置为 RootSession 当前文件模型。

而 **race** 指的是扫描和 mutation 的完成顺序不确定。

## 建议解决方式

每个 Snapshot 有版本号：

```text
generation = 10
```

扫描开始时记录当前版本：

```text
ScanTask {
  base_generation = 10
}
```

之后 rename 成功，Core 发布新模型：

```text
generation = 11
```

旧扫描完成时检查：

```text
scan.base_generation == current_generation

10 == 11  // false
```

于是旧结果不能发布：

```text
丢弃旧扫描结果
重新扫描受影响目录
```

完整顺序：

```text
scan 开始：       generation 10
rename 完成：     generation 11
scan 尝试发布：   发现 10 != 11
处理：            不发布，重新 scoped scan
```

## Mutation 正在执行时也不能发布扫描结果

例如一个 plan 包含：

```text
rename A -> temp
rename B -> A
rename temp -> B
```

扫描如果刚好在中间运行，可能看到：

```text
temp
A
```

这是临时状态，不能作为正式 Snapshot 发布。

因此还需要一个简单状态：

```text
mutation_active = true | false
```

发布扫描结果必须同时满足：

```text
扫描开始时的 generation 仍是当前 generation
扫描期间没有发生 mutation
当前没有 mutation 正在执行
```

否则就放弃这个扫描结果，等 mutation 完成后重新扫描。

## 可以换一个更直白的名称

文档中不必继续用 `scan/mutation publication race`，可以改成：

> **防止旧扫描结果覆盖新的文件操作结果**

本质上只是确保：

```text
新完成的 rename/move/create/delete
永远不会被更早启动的扫描结果覆盖
```
ok ,
